mnemonics: Think of a blockchain as a digital LEGO tower: each new block (brick) is cryptographically attached (snapped) to the one before it, making the entire chain (tower) very hard to topple or alter.
keyPoints: A blockchain is a distributed, immutable digital ledger of transactions.
Data is stored in cryptographically linked blocks, forming a chain.
Decentralization and consensus mechanisms ensure trust without a central authority.
commonMistakes: Assuming blockchain is only about cryptocurrency; it is the underlying technology for many other applications.
Confusing immutability with being un-hackable; while extremely difficult to alter, the chain can be attacked (e.g., 51% attack).
Equating a distributed ledger with a blockchain; a blockchain is a type of distributed ledger, but not all distributed ledgers are blockchains.
